title: Analyst Assurance Workstation
public_claim: An analyst workstation for AI-augmented intelligence analysis.
distinctive_mechanism: It converts AI support into inspection surfaces: knowledge production, prompt engineering discipline, cognitive-bias safeguards, Key Assumptions Check, live SAT visualization instruments, source review, ICD/NIST-bound assurance language, analyst-model disagreement, and human acceptance or correction before conclusions move forward. The cross-page workstation can connect AI Governance Framework Navigator, EU AI Act considerations, AI System Assessment, AI Failure Mode Taxonomy, OSINT Narrative Mapping, OSIRIS LLM, and per-stage AI/HITL/ICD/NIST checks.

flagship_demos.title: Analyst workstation human-AI delta
flagship_demos.why_distinctive: The proof must show whether structured human analysis changed, constrained, corrected, or blocked the AI output.

fixture.question: An intelligence-style analytic question with ambiguous evidence.
fixture.human_review: structured analyst correction using SAT method and confidence rule

runbook: Produce or supply initial AI answer.
Separate evidence, assumptions, and missing information.
Run one structured analytic technique.
Compare human judgment against AI output.
Record accept, qualify, block, rerun, or quarantine decision.
acceptance_criteria: The demo shows what changed because of human structured analysis.
The final output is not simply AI prose with a human label.
Confidence and limits are explicit.

component_demo_cases.claim_to_demonstrate: selects the structured analytic technique or technique sequence that fits the task rather than applying SAT labels ornamentally
component_demo_cases.input_fixture: intelligence or research question, decision context, evidence type, uncertainty level, and user role
component_demo_cases.operation_under_test: selects the structured analytic technique or technique sequence that fits the task rather than applying SAT labels ornamentally
component_demo_cases.expected_output: method assignment and technique rationale as a reviewable artifact
component_demo_cases.evidence_locator: Expected proof artifact: method assignment and technique rationale as a reviewable artifact
runbook: build or run a demonstration that produces the specified output
Use this input fixture: intelligence or research question, decision context, evidence type, uncertainty level, and user role
Verify that the component produces: method assignment and technique rationale as a reviewable artifact
Preserve the evidence at or beside: Expected proof artifact: method assignment and technique rationale as a reviewable artifact
acceptance_criteria: The input, operation, and expected output are all visible.
The evidence locator is present or the missing evidence is explicitly named.
The result supports the component claim without implying broader completion.
component_demo_cases.claim_ceiling: The page may claim proof-structured components and visible verification targets. It may not claim external validation, production deployment, or completed empirical findings unless the project-specific record states those facts.
component_demo_cases.next_build_task: Run one analytic question through Task And Method Assignment; confirm evidence, assumptions, method decision, confidence limit, and human/AI delta.
